chunks.text: Anthony D. Joseph is a Chancellor's Professor at UC Berkeley. His primary research interests are in Genomics, Secure Machine Learning, Datacenters, mobile/distributed computing, and wireless communications (networking and telephony). He is part of the RISE Lab. Education: 1998, Ph.D., Computer Science, MIT; 1988, S.M./S.B., Electrical Engineering and Computer Science/Computer Science and Engineering, MIT.
chunks.question: What are Anthony Joseph's main research interests?
chunks.answer: Genomics, secure machine learning, and distributed systems.

chunks.text: Research Areas: Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT), Security (SEC). Specific interests include Computer and Network Security; Distributed systems; Mobile computing; Wireless networking; Software engineering, and operating systems. Research Centers: EPIC Data lab (EPIC).
chunks.question: Which research areas does Anthony Joseph focus on?

chunks.text: Selected Publications: M. Barreno et al., 'Can Machine Learning Be Secure? (Invited Talk),' 2006 ACM Symp. on Information, Computer and Communications Security. R. Bajcsy et al., 'Cyber defense technology networking and evaluation,' Communications of the ACM, 2004. B. Y. Zhao et al., 'Tapestry: A resilient global-scale overlay for service deployment,' IEEE J. Selected Areas in Communications, 2004.
chunks.question: What is the title of Joseph's 2006 publication on machine learning?

chunks.text: Additional Publications: A. Konrad et al., 'A Markov-based channel model algorithm for wireless networks,' Wireless Networks, 2003. B. Y. Zhao et al., 'Brocade: Landmark routing on overlay networks,' 2002. T. D. Hodes et al., 'An architecture for secure wide-area service discovery,' Wireless Networks, 2002. S. Q. Zhuang et al., 'Bayeux: An architecture for scalable and fault-tolerant wide-area data dissemination,' 2001.
chunks.question: What algorithm did Konrad et al. develop in 2003?
chunks.answer: Markov-based channel model for wireless networks.

chunks.text: Further Publications: B. Y. Zhao et al., 'Tapestry: An Infrastructure for Fault-Resilient Wide-Area Location and Routing,' Tech. Rep. UCB/CSD-01-1141, 2001. S. D. Gribble et al., 'The Ninja architecture for robust internet-scale systems and services,' Computer Networks, 2001. H. J. Wang et al., 'ICEBERG: An Internet core network architecture for integrated communications,' IEEE Personal Communications, 2000.
chunks.question: What is the Ninja architecture designed for?

chunks.text: Selected Honors and Awards: Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching Computer Science, 2007. NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER), 2000. Okawa Research Grant, 1999.
chunks.question: Which teaching award did Anthony Joseph receive in 2007?
chunks.answer: Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence.
